original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: Royal College of Emergency Medicine and Royal College of General Practitioners
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adult and pediatric patients being discharged directly from an emergency department.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Emergency-department discharge communication with patients and primary care.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for recording the clinician-confirmed outcome, treatment plan, information communicated, required follow-up, responsible party, discharge destination and safety-net discussion. It does not make the discharge decision or generate advice.

exact_sections.heading: Summary and Patient Communication
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.

exact_sections.heading: GP Communication and example discharge-letter headings
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

title: Discharge to General Practice
publisher: Royal College of Emergency Medicine and Royal College of General Practitioners
jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adult and pediatric patients being discharged directly from an emergency department.
intended_setting: Emergency-department discharge communication with patients and primary care.
